quiz.title: Algebra 2: Unit 11 Quiz 10
quiz.subtitle: 11.10: Modeling with Trigonometric Functions
quiz.subject: Mathematics
quiz.topic: Creating functions from real-world periodic data

questions.question: An oceanographer measures the tide. The high tide is $12$ feet and the low tide is $2$ feet. What is the midline ($k$) of the trigonometric model?
questions.explanation: The midline is the average of the max and min: $(12 + 2)/2 = 7$ feet.

questions.question: Using the tide data (Max: $12$ ft, Min: $2$ ft), what is the amplitude ($a$) of the model?
questions.explanation: Amplitude is the distance from the midline to the max: $12 - 7 = 5$ feet, or $(12-2)/2 = 5$.

questions.question: A Ferris wheel has a diameter of $40$ meters and the bottom of the wheel is $2$ meters off the ground. What is the maximum height reached by a rider?
questions.explanation: Max height = Diameter + Height of bottom. $40 + 2 = 42$ meters.

questions.question: The temperature fluctuates between $60^{\circ}$F and $90^{\circ}$F every $24$ hours. Low occurs at midnight ($t=0$). Which function models this?
questions.explanation: Midline $= 75$, Amplitude $= 15$. Period $= 24$, so $b = 2\pi/24 = \pi/12$. Negative cosine starts at the minimum.

questions.question: A buoy completes a full cycle every $10$ seconds. What is the value of $b$ for the trigonometric model?
A predator-prey model shows the population oscillating between $100$ and $500$. What is the amplitude of this function?
A population oscillates between $200$ and $600$ with a period of $8$ years. At $t=0$, it is at its minimum. Write the equation using cosine.
questions.explanation: Midline $= 400$, Amplitude $= 200$. Period $8 \to b = \pi/4$. Starts at min $\to$ negative cosine: $y = -200\cos(\frac{\pi}{4}t) + 400$.

questions.question: A person's blood pressure is modeled by $P(t) = 20\sin(160\pi t) + 100$. What is the systolic (maximum) blood pressure?
A Ferris wheel completes one revolution every $60$ seconds. Rider starts at the very bottom ($2$ m) at $t=0$, midline is $22$ m. What is the height at $t=15$ seconds?
questions.explanation: At $15$ seconds, the rider has completed $1/4$ of a cycle. From the bottom, $1/4$ cycle reaches the midline: $22$ meters.

questions.question: A light signal flashes every $0.2$ seconds. What is the frequency in flashes per second (Hz)?

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You can build trig models from real-world data — finding midline, amplitude, period, frequency, writing equations with correct phase shifts, and evaluating at specific times.
scoreMessages.70: Good job. Key reminders: (1) Midline = (max + min)/2, (2) Amplitude = (max − min)/2, (3) $b = 2\pi/T$ where $T$ is the period, (4) Start at max $\to$ cosine; start at min $\to$ negative cosine; start at midline going up $\to$ sine.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing. Focus on: (1) Calculating midline and amplitude from max/min values, (2) Converting period to $b$ using $b = 2\pi/T$, (3) Choosing sine or cosine based on the starting position, (4) Writing the full equation with all parameters.
scoreMessages.0: Review trig modeling: (1) $y = a\sin(b(t-h)) + k$ or $y = a\cos(b(t-h)) + k$, (2) $k = (\text{max} + \text{min})/2$, (3) $|a| = (\text{max} - \text{min})/2$, (4) $b = 2\pi/T$, (5) Start at midline going up $\to$ sine; start at max $\to$ cosine; start at min $\to$ negative cosine.
